questions.question: 1. A transmission line of length more than 160 Km and line voltage higher 100 kV then it is called:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Short lines are less than 80 km.
explanations.c: Correct. Lines longer than 160 km and above 100 kV are considered long transmission lines.

questions.question: 2. If the overhead transmission line is loaded with the loading equal to the surge impedance, then the voltage at receiving end is:

explanations.a: Incorrect. That happens under light load (Ferranti effect).
explanations.c: Correct. When loaded with surge impedance (Zc), the line is flat-compensated and Vs = Vr.

questions.question: 3. The parameters of a transmission line for exact solution are:

explanations.a: Correct. Exact solution of long transmission lines requires considering distributed parameters (R, L, C, G per unit length).
explanations.b: Incorrect. Lumped parameters are used for approximate solutions (short/medium lines).

questions.question: 4. The value of charging current at the receiving end of the transmission line is:

explanations.a: Correct. At the receiving end, charging current is zero because the line is open at that point for charging current consideration.

questions.question: 5. The square root of the ratio of line impedance to its shunt admittance is called:

answers.b: Surge impedance of the transmission line
answers.d: Surge admittance of the transmission line

explanations.a: Incorrect. Regulation is voltage drop percentage.
